title: James DiNicolantonio: supplementation and nutrient deficiencies, eggs vs. bagels, creatine and diet

chunks.title: Introduction
chunks.summary: The podcast host introduces James DiNicolantonio, highlighting his expertise and recent publications, including 'The Collagen Cure'. They discuss a variety of topics related to nutrition, exercise, and health.
chunks.transcript: if people can't wrap their heads around consuming glucose we'll think about consuming how much glycogen you would get when you would consume on this week's podcast I had the distinct pleasure of chatting with my buddy James and nickel Antonio who is a wealth of knowledge he's written so many books I believe his last one is called the collagen cure we talk about all kinds of things I ask him what he feels about carbs and keto and magnesium and hydration and salt and electrolytes and we talk about all kinds of stuff so James is a wealth of information you will enjoy this podcast with him I know you'll get a lot out of it and look for our collabs on social media to promote this one so enjoy this

chunks.title: Visible Abs and Statistics
chunks.summary: The conversation shifts to the statistics around body fat and visible abs, comparing how rare it is to achieve visible abs versus becoming a millionaire. James discusses the implications of body fat percentage and societal perceptions of fitness.
chunks.transcript: Antonio he's a pharmacist and you guys are probably familiar with him on Instagram he's a uh he's a tweet Master James thanks for coming on the podcast man it's been way too long thanks for having me back so I going through some of your prolific tweets if you're like the Tweet master and um I pulled out some highlights that I thought might be fun to start the podcast with so one of the one of the cooler ones I saw which was very popular was this notion that having visible ABS having a six-pack is harder than becoming a millionaire where did you find the statistics around this this is crazy yeah well so essentially in order for you to be able to see have visible ABS you have to have at a minimum 14 body fat or so when you have 70 of us adults being overweight or obese it's pretty rare to find someone with a 6 anything less than yeah somebody less than 14 body fat so the numbers in that post were that three million people in the United States which is what 330 million humans so 3 million people have visible abs and there's 22 million people with a net worth of more than a million dollars so we're talking like six seven X here that's crazy yeah exactly like essentially one percent of the US population has the body fat necessary to even have visible ads oh my gosh

chunks.title: Eggs vs. Bagels Study
chunks.summary: The duo discusses a study comparing the effects of eating eggs for breakfast versus bagels on weight loss and calorie consumption. They address the misconceptions surrounding egg consumption and TMAO levels.

chunks.title: Magnesium Deficiency
chunks.summary: James highlights the prevalence of magnesium deficiency in the population, its health implications, and the potential symptoms associated with low magnesium levels. They also discuss food sources and supplementation.

chunks.title: Final Thoughts and Book Promotion
chunks.summary: The podcast concludes with James sharing where listeners can find him and learn more about his work, particularly his latest book, 'The Collagen Cure'. They encourage healthy discussions on nutrition.
